title: AGENTS.md
text: package exposes vendored Rust zmij crate extendr. format_double() accepts numeric vectors, matrices, arrays. parse_double() accepts character vectors, matrices, arrays. Preserve names, dimensions, dimension names, copy classes unrelated attributes across type conversion. Preserve R missing values distinguish NA_real_ NaN. Non-finite values format "NaN", "inf", "-inf". zmij uses fixed notation decimal exponents -5 15 scientific notation outside range. Scientific exponents include plus sign positive. Preserve signed zero ("-0.0"). Verify round trips parse_double(), .double(). Base R decimal parsing can fail near binary64 boundaries platforms; observed .Machine$double.xmin, .Machine$double.xmax, extreme values R 4.6 macOS.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Put public validation, shape restoration, roxygen2 documentation R/. Keep extendr-facing implementations src/rust/src/lib.rs. Rust functions exposed implementation details must use @keywords internal. Register every new Rust function extendr_module! block. R/extendr-wrappers.R src/entrypoint.c generated artifacts. edit hand. local Rust package upstream crate named zmij. Keep dependency aliased zmij-rs Cargo.toml. Upstream zmij 1.0.23 requires Rust 1.71. Keep Cargo rust-version, DESCRIPTION SystemRequirements, Cargo.lock, vendor archive synchronized.

title: Round-Trip-Safe Double-Precision Formatting
text: zmij converts double-precision floating-point values decimal strings shortest significands needed round-trip recovery. provides vectorized R interface zmij Rust crate, port Victor Zverovich’s zmij algorithm.

title: Round-Trip-Safe Double-Precision Formatting
text: can install development version zmij GitHub :

title: Round-Trip-Safe Double-Precision Formatting
text: Format numeric vector: Missing non-finite values handled explicitly: parse_double() also useful directly correctly rounded conversion important, especially near limits binary64 range: Names, dimensions, values preserved round trip:
code: library(zmij) x <- c(pi, 0.1, -0, .Machine$double.xmin, .Machine$double.xmax) x |> format_double() #> [1] "3.141592653589793"       "0.1"                     #> [3] "-0.0"                    "2.2250738585072014e-308" #> [5] "1.7976931348623157e+308" x |> format_double() |> parse_double() |> identical(x) #> [1] TRUE format_double(c(NA_real_, NaN, Inf, -Inf)) #> [1] NA     "NaN"  "inf"  "-inf" parse_double(c("1.5", "3e-1", "5e-324", "1.7976931348623157e+308")) #> [1]  1.500000e+00  3.000000e-01 4.940656e-324 1.797693e+308 x <- c(pi = pi, tenth = 0.1) x |> format_double() #>                  pi               tenth  #> "3.141592653589793"               "0.1" x |> format_double() |> parse_double() |> identical(x) #> [1] TRUE  x <- matrix(   c(0.1, pi, 1e-6, 1e16),   nrow = 2,   dimnames = list(c("first", "second"), c("small", "large")) ) x |> format_double() #>        small               large   #> first  "0.1"               "1e-6"  #> second "3.141592653589793" "1e+16" x |> format_double() |> parse_double() |> identical(x) #> [1] TRUE

what: Format Double-Precision Values with zmij — format_double
title: Format Double-Precision Values with zmij — format_double
text: Convert numeric values decimal strings shortest significands recover original IEEE 754 binary64 values parsed using round--nearest, ties--even rounding.

title: Format Double-Precision Values with zmij — format_double
Format Double-Precision Values with zmij — format_double
text: x numeric vector, matrix, array.

title: Format Double-Precision Values with zmij — format_double
text: character vector length shape x. Names dimensions preserved. Missing values remain NA; NaN, positive infinity, negative infinity become "NaN", "inf", "-inf", respectively.

title: Format Double-Precision Values with zmij — format_double
text: Integer inputs converted double precision formatting. zmij uses fixed notation decimal exponents -5 15 scientific notation outside range. attributes, including classes, retained returned values character strings.

title: Format Double-Precision Values with zmij — format_double
text: Zverovich V (2025). "Faster double--string conversion." https://vitaut.net/posts/2025/faster-dtoa/. Tolnay D. "zmij: double--string conversion algorithm based Schubfach." https://github.com/dtolnay/zmij.

title: Format Double-Precision Values with zmij — format_double
code: x <- c(pi, 0.1, -0, .Machine$double.xmin, .Machine$double.xmax) formatted <- format_double(x) formatted #> [1] "3.141592653589793"       "0.1"                     #> [3] "-0.0"                    "2.2250738585072014e-308" #> [5] "1.7976931348623157e+308" identical(parse_double(formatted), x) #> [1] TRUE  format_double(c(NA_real_, NaN, Inf, -Inf)) #> [1] NA     "NaN"  "inf"  "-inf"

title: Parse Double-Precision Strings — parse_double
text: Parse decimal strings IEEE 754 binary64 values using Rust's correctly rounded parser. inverse format_double() every finite double.

title: Parse Double-Precision Strings — parse_double
text: x character vector, matrix, array.

title: Parse Double-Precision Strings — parse_double
text: double vector length shape x. Names dimensions preserved. Missing strings remain NA_real_.

title: Parse Double-Precision Strings — parse_double
text: parser accepts decimal scientific notation, along "NaN", "inf", "-inf". Leading trailing whitespace invalid strings cause error identifies first invalid element. Using parse_double() avoids platform-specific edge cases R's built-decimal parser near limits binary64 range.

title: Parse Double-Precision Strings — parse_double
code: x <- c(pi, 0.1, -0, .Machine$double.xmin, .Machine$double.xmax) identical(parse_double(format_double(x)), x) #> [1] TRUE  parse_double(c("1.5", "3e-1", NA_character_, "inf")) #> [1] 1.5 0.3  NA Inf

title: zmij 0.1.0
text: Added format_double() vectorized, round-trip-safe formatting double-precision values using vendored Rust zmij implementation (#1). Added parse_double() correctly rounded conversion decimal strings back double-precision values (#1). Numeric character vectors, matrices, arrays supported. Names, dimensions, dimension names preserved, explicit handling missing values, NaN, infinities, signed zero, empty inputs.
